title: Building an Adaptive Parallel Channel Detection and Breakout System in MQL5
author: Christian Benjamin (lynnchris)
verdict: HIGH EXTRACT — Parallel channel = parallel trendlines. MaxSlopeDifference for slope similarity. Liquidity sweep rejection (wick out + close back = false break). Retest mode = our zone retest confirmation logic. Direct upgrade to EntryEngine zone break confirmation.

channel_detection.step_1: Find swing highs and swing lows with ATR filter
channel_detection.step_2: Try all pairs of highs for upper boundary, pairs of lows for lower boundary
channel_detection.step_4: Accept if both boundaries have >= MinTouchPointsRequired touches within TouchToleranceATR
channel_detection.step_5: Score by touch count; keep highest-scoring channel per lookback window

MaxSlopeDifference.description: Max slope divergence between upper/lower boundaries

MinChannelWidthATR.description: Minimum channel width in ATR units

LookbackBars.description: Historical bars to search for channels

liquidity_sweep_rejection.rule: Wick beyond boundary + close back inside = liquidity sweep, NOT a breakout
liquidity_sweep_rejection.implementation: if bar.high > channel_top AND bar.close < channel_top: mark as sweep, reject breakout

retest_mode.logic: Price breaks boundary -> PENDING state -> if price returns to near boundary and bounces -> CONFIRMED

keep: Liquidity sweep rejection (wick + close back = false break)
discard: Full channel detection (use zone system instead)
improve: Apply liquidity sweep rejection to all zone invalidations in EntryEngine
Retest mode: after zone break, wait for price to return to zone before entry
MaxSlopeDifference validates if two trendlines are truly parallel (channel vs wedge)
